core_mechanism: universality — the collapse of systems with different microscopic interactions onto a single interaction-detail-independent description in a limiting regime, so that results derived for one system transfer to another governed by the same few dominant scales/parameters
brief_reason: The abstract argues that despite nuclei having far more complicated interactions than ultracold gases, 'universal results from atomic physics should have impact in certain limits of the nuclear domain,' i.e. shared behavior emerges in a limit independent of microscopic detail.
